_meta.pricing_note: USD per 1M tokens, approximate as of 2026-Q1. Prices change frequently — verify at provider pricing pages before budgeting.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.6

pricing.note: Most expensive Claude; reserve for tasks where quality justifies cost

best_for: Tasks where errors are very costly

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6

pricing.note: Best price-capability ratio in the Claude family for engineering tasks

avoid_for: Extreme cost sensitivity at very high volume

pricing.note: Cheapest Claude; excellent value for high-volume, low-complexity tasks

models.name: GPT-4o

pricing.note: Strong value for multimodal tasks; audio in/out included in standard pricing

avoid_for: Privacy-sensitive or regulated data without enterprise DPA
EU data sovereignty without Azure OpenAI EU region

pricing.note: Extended thinking output is billed at output rates; total cost per task can be very high

models.name: o4-mini

pricing.note: Best cost-efficiency among reasoning models; ~10x cheaper than o3

best_for: Tasks needing extended reasoning at lower cost than o3
Middle ground between Sonnet and o3

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro

pricing.note: Context >200K billed at higher rates; 1M context tasks can be expensive

best_for: Huge context windows (entire repos, long documents)
Tasks where breadth is the primary constraint
avoid_for: Privacy-sensitive or regulated data without explicit Cloud DPA and EU region

pricing.note: Extremely low cost; 1M context sweep costs cents; best throughput/dollar of any listed model

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B

pricing.note: No API cost; infra cost varies by hardware. Via third-party APIs (Groq, Together, etc.): ~$0.06-0.80/1M tokens depending on provider.

avoid_for: Cases where self-hosting cost exceeds privacy requirement

pricing.note: EU-based pricing in EUR also available; Le Chat Enterprise has volume discounts

models.name: DeepSeek V3
models.governance_warning: G:1 — Chinese jurisdiction. Never use for PII, PHI, confidential IP, credentials, regulated data, or government/defense work.

pricing.note: Exceptional price-capability ratio; cheapest frontier-class model. G:1 severely limits safe use cases.

best_for: Non-sensitive tasks where cost is dominant constraint
avoid_for: Any sensitive, personal, regulated, or confidential data

models.name: DeepSeek R1
models.governance_warning: G:1 — Chinese jurisdiction. Never use for PII, PHI, confidential IP, credentials, regulated data, or government/defense work.

pricing.note: ~20x cheaper than o3 for comparable reasoning; G:1 severely limits safe use cases.

best_for: Hard reasoning and math on non-sensitive public data
avoid_for: Any sensitive, personal, regulated, or confidential data

models.name: MiniMax Text-01
models.governance_warning: Chinese company. Via MiniMax API: G:1 (same caution as DeepSeek). Self-hosted open weights raise governance to G:3 but no compliance certs available.

pricing.note: 1M context at very low cost; open weights available for self-hosting

best_for: Long document analysis where cost matters
Cheap alternative to Gemini 2.5 Pro for context-heavy work
avoid_for: Tasks requiring strong reasoning or coding capability

models.name: Qwen 2.5 72B
models.governance_warning: Via Alibaba Cloud API: G:1 (Chinese jurisdiction). Self-hosted (Apache 2.0 license): governance rises to G:4 — no data leaves your infra, but no compliance certs from Alibaba.

pricing.note: Self-hosted: GPU infra cost only. Via Together/Groq/etc.: ~$0.20–0.90/1M. Alibaba Cloud API available but G:1.

best_for: Open-source alternative to GPT-4o class for non-sensitive work
avoid_for: Tasks requiring frontier coding or multimodal
Any use via Alibaba Cloud API with sensitive data

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B
models.governance_warning: Via Alibaba Cloud API: G:1. Self-hosted (Apache 2.0): G:4. Recommended deployment is self-hosted for any proprietary code.

pricing.note: Smaller than 72B so cheaper to host; very cost-effective coding model

best_for: Self-hosted coding at very low cost
Code-first pipelines on proprietary code when self-hosted
avoid_for: Non-coding tasks where a general model is better

models.name: Phi-4

pricing.note: MIT license; cheapest STEM-capable model; via Azure AI Foundry with enterprise DPA available

best_for: Fast math/science Q&A where context window is not a constraint
avoid_for: Any task needing broad knowledge base

models.name: Gemma 3 27B
models.governance_warning: Via Google API: G:2 (same as Gemini). Self-hosted open weights (Gemma Terms of Use): G:4 — data stays local, strong sovereignty, but no Anthropic-tier compliance certs.

pricing.note: Open weights under Gemma Terms; self-hosting recommended for proprietary workloads

best_for: EU deployment with full data sovereignty
avoid_for: Tasks needing cutting-edge reasoning or coding

models.name: Grok 3

pricing.note: Available via xAI API and X Premium subscription; real-time web access available in some modes

best_for: Real-time data access via web search mode
Alternative frontier model when Anthropic/OpenAI are rate-limited

models.name: Command R+

pricing.note: Weights available for self-hosting; Cohere is SOC 2 certified; HIPAA BAA available for enterprise

best_for: Enterprise workloads needing SOC 2 + HIPAA without full self-hosting

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash

pricing.note: Updated Flash variant of Gemini 2.5; slightly stronger than Flash 2.0 on instruction following

best_for: High-volume multimodal, 1M context at low cost
Faster alternative to Gemini 2.5 Pro for non-sensitive work

models.name: Codestral

pricing.note: Mistral's code-focused model; weights available for self-hosting; strong fill-in-the-middle

best_for: Self-hosted coding at lower cost than Claude Sonnet

models.name: Mistral Medium 3

pricing.note: Between Small and Large; good balance of speed and capability

best_for: Budget-constrained tasks needing more than Mistral Small

pricing.note: Mistral's entry-level model; very cheap, EU-based; good for classification and routing

models.name: Mistral Deep Think

pricing.note: Mistral's reasoning-mode model; EU-based alternative to o3/DeepSeek R1 for regulated data

best_for: Extended reasoning on EU-governed or GDPR-sensitive data
Alternative to o3/DeepSeek R1 when G:4 is required

models.name: Qwen3 235B (MoE)
models.governance_warning: Via Alibaba Cloud API: G:1. Self-hosted (Apache 2.0): G:4.

pricing.note: Massive MoE (235B params, 22B active); open weights; GPU cluster required for self-hosting. Via third-party APIs: pricing varies (~$0.50-2.00/1M).

best_for: Self-hosted alternative to Claude Opus/GPT-5 where G:4 is sufficient
avoid_for: Via Alibaba API for any sensitive data

pricing.note: ESTIMATED — scores based on extrapolation from GPT-4o trajectory. Run Workflow C to validate.

best_for: Hardest tasks requiring SOTA across all dimensions (estimated)

best_for: Frontier reasoning with real-time data access (estimated)

pricing.note: ESTIMATED — major xAI release. Run Workflow C to validate.
ESTIMATED — minor update to Grok 4. Run Workflow C to validate.

models.name: Grok 4 Heavy

pricing.note: ESTIMATED — compute-heavy reasoning variant analogous to o3. Run Workflow C to validate.

best_for: Hardest reasoning tasks requiring deep compute (estimated)
Alternative to o3 with real-time data

models.name: Gemini 3 Flash

pricing.note: ESTIMATED — next-gen Google Flash. Run Workflow C to validate.

best_for: Massive context + multimodal at minimal cost (estimated)

pricing.note: ESTIMATED — next-gen Google Pro. Run Workflow C to validate.

models.name: MiniMax M2.5
models.governance_warning: Chinese company. Via MiniMax API: G:1. Self-hosted open weights raise governance to G:3.

pricing.note: ESTIMATED — updated MiniMax. Run Workflow C to validate.

best_for: 1M context at low cost, non-sensitive data (estimated)
